questions.question: Order the stages of the full research cycle as practised in the course.
questions.explanation: The full research cycle runs Research Question, then design, then data, then analysis, and finally the paper.

questions.question: Order the project workflow from topic selection to submission.
questions.explanation: The workflow goes topic selection, proposal, ethics and consent, data collection, analysis, then paper, with ethics cleared before any data is collected.

questions.question: Order the plenary lecture topics as the course presents them.
questions.explanation: The plenary lectures progress from RQ and literature review, to experimental design, to data analysis, and finally writing.

questions.question: Order the high-level project timeline milestones.
questions.explanation: The timeline moves from topic selection to proposal, then data collection, then analysis, and ends with the paper.

questions.question: Order these early group-formation and planning steps in the order a student should do them.
items: Pick a supervisor topic from the menu
Self-enroll in the research-topic group on Canvas
correct_order: Pick a supervisor topic from the menu
Self-enroll in the research-topic group on Canvas
questions.explanation: A student first picks a supervisor topic, then self-enrolls in that research-topic group on Canvas (not the auto-assigned group), and then the formed team signs its Team Expectations Agreement.
